{{Infobox character
| name = Judge Suriel
| aliases = Suriel’s Presence, Suriel
| abilities = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|sacred art to reverse time, [[Presence]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|, can anchor a world to order through her connection to the Way}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|, can pass through the Way}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|, can look into Fate}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|, can tear open a rift into the Way}}
| titles = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[The Phoenix|the Phoenix]], Sixth Judge of the Abidan Court, [[judge|Judge]], [[celestial messenger]], [[Phoenix]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|, [[Hound]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|, leader of the Abidan}}
| affiliations = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Abidan Court]], [[Abidan]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|, [[The Eight-Man Empire|Eight-Man Empire]]}}
| professions = {{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|healer, [[judge|Judge]]}}{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|, caretaker}}
| residence = {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Seat of the Accused]]}}
| appears_in = Cradle
| introduced = [[Wintersteel (novel)|Wintersteel]]
}}

''See also: [[Judge Suriel Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel, also known as the [[Phoenix]], is the Sixth [[Judge]] of the [[Abidan Court]]. Recognized as the greatest healer in existence, she possesses a unique role within the [[Court]] of Seven, often acting as a bridge between other [[Judges]] like Ozriel and [[Makiel]] to promote cooperation. Her identity and authority were passed down from a predecessor, along with her signature weapon, Suriel’s [[Razor]]—a powerful instrument designed for healing and precision rather than mere destruction. She is also characterized by her Mantle, a flowing river of white flame that serves as a source of power. Physically, Suriel has undergone self-alteration to enhance her performance, appearing as a perfected version of her former self with green hair, seamless white armor, and purple eyes containing complex formations that allow her to perceive [[Fate]]. She is accompanied by a [[Presence]], a spectral [[construct]] that assists with information processing and communication. Suriel's abilities allow her to manipulate the [[Way]] to restore order, heal wounds, and even reconstitute entire continents or reverse cosmic destruction. Despite her immense power and status, she is noted for her compassion, a trait sometimes viewed as a weakness by others in the [[Abidan]].}}

== Unsouled ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel is a celestial messenger and the Sixth [[Judge]] of the [[Abidan Court]], designated as zero-zero-six. A [[Phoenix]] with the power of healing, she possesses a pale face, green hair, purple eyes containing rings of [[fate]]-seeing symbols, and transparent gray ghostlines stretching from her skull to her fingers. She wears a seamless white uniform of inch-thick liquid armor that resembles [[Forged madra]] and carries a meter-long blue [[steel]] shaft that she can banish to [[Sanctum]]. Utilizing her [[Presence]], she can observe locations from a thousand kilometers away to retrieve and transmit reports through words, images, and impressions. During her interactions, she provided [[Lindon]] with visions and a token tied to him by unbreakable strings of [[fate]] that she can sense across time and worlds. Additionally, she is capable of using a sacred art to reverse time.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel is a healer and the [[Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6">''Unsouled'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel possesses transparent gray ghostlines running from the back of her head down to her fingers.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel wears a seamless white uniform made of inch-thick liquid armor.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel's liquid armor could be mistaken for [[Forged madra]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel possesses a meter-long shaft of blue [[steel]] weapon that she can banish to [[Sanctum]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel's eyes contain a ring of symbols that function as tools to help her see the flow of [[fate]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel can observe target locations from a thousand kilometers away using her [[Presence]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11">''Unsouled'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel is a white-armored woman with green hair and ghostly lines leading from her fingertips to her skull.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel is a celestial messenger.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel uses a sacred art to reverse time.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel possesses green hair and a pale face.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel has purple eyes.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel's organization refers to the world as '[[Cradle]]'.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel gives [[Lindon]] a token that she can sense across worlds and beyond time.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Suriel's token]] is tied to [[Lindon]] with strings of [[fate]] and cannot be broken or lost.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel provided [[Lindon]] with visions.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c16">''Unsouled'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel is a [[Phoenix]] and the Sixth [[Judge]] of the [[Abidan Court]].<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20">''Unsouled'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|[[Suriel's Presence]] retrieves and transmits reports as words, images, and impressions.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel's designation is zero-zero-six.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-01-unsouled|Suriel has the power of healing.<ref name="cradle-01-unsouled-c20" />}}

== Soulsmith ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Suriel is a character who spoke to [[Lindon]] about his timeline.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c2">''Soulsmith'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Suriel suggested how far [[Lindon]] could travel down his [[Path]].<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-02-soulsmith|Suriel challenged [[Lindon]] to reach great heights.<ref name="cradle-02-soulsmith-c16">''Soulsmith'', chapter 16</ref>}}

== Blackflame ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Known as the [[Phoenix]], Suriel is an [[Abidan]] who possesses the ability to anchor a world to order via her connection to the [[Way]]. During her interactions with [[Lindon]], she revealed knowledge of the [[Sword Sage]] and showed him a [[dragon]] residing beneath the sea. She is associated with the [[Eight]]-Man Empire, has ties to the ancestors of the [[Arelius family]], and serves as a lodger for [[Yerin]]. Having witnessed the death of an [[Iteration]], she remains a figure of significant cosmic perspective.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|[[Lindon]] has seen Suriel with his own eyes.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c2">''Blackflame'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel is associated with the [[Eight]]-Man Empire.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c5">''Blackflame'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel mentioned the [[Sword Sage]] to [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c7">''Blackflame'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel showed [[Lindon]] a [[dragon]] beneath the sea.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c8">''Blackflame'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel is an [[Abidan]] who can anchor a world to order through her connection to the [[Way]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13">''Blackflame'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel is known as the [[Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c13" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel is a 'lodger' associated with [[Yerin]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c15">''Blackflame'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel witnessed the death of an [[Iteration]].<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21">''Blackflame'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-03-blackflame|Suriel was familiar with the [[Arelius family]] and their ancestors.<ref name="cradle-03-blackflame-c21" />}}

== Skysworn ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Suriel told [[Lindon]] he had thirty years.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c1">''Skysworn'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Suriel is a member of the [[Court]].<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c2">''Skysworn'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Suriel has demonstrated absolute authority.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c12">''Skysworn'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-04-skysworn|Suriel is an [[Abidan]] who stands over the sixth division and has purple eyes and emerald hair.<ref name="cradle-04-skysworn-c18">''Skysworn'', chapter 18</ref>}}

== Underlord ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|As a [[Judge]], Suriel arrived clad in seamless white armor and wielding the weapon known as Suriel’s [[Razor]]. During the course of battle, this protective armor sustained cracks. Following these events, [[Lindon]] found himself in a position where he owed Suriel his life. Suriel eventually showed [[Lindon]] the [[Sacred Valley]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Suriel's armor is seamless and white.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c1">''Underlord'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Suriel possesses Suriel’s [[Razor]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Suriel is a [[Judge]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Suriel's armor cracked during the battle.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|[[Lindon]] owes Suriel his life.<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c8">''Underlord'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-06-underlord|Suriel showed [[Lindon]] the [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-06-underlord-c15">''Underlord'', chapter 15</ref>}}

== Uncrowned ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-07-uncrowned|Suriel reported a missing world to [[Makiel]].<ref name="cradle-07-uncrowned-c2">''Uncrowned'', chapter 2</ref>}}

== Wintersteel ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Judge]] Suriel's visit proved to be a significant event for [[Lindon]], featuring a room he had previously seen in one of her visions years earlier. During their encounter, she offered [[Lindon]] a second chance and reminded him of her belief that all [[Paths]] ultimately boil down to the singular goal of self-improvement. Her visit also included a vision of the future depicting something massive trampling through [[Sacred Valley]]. While it is suggested she may have sent [[Kiuran]] to eliminate [[Lindon]] from contention, she remains a favorite of an individual noted by the [[Abidan]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Lindon]] had seen the room in Suriel's vision years before.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2">''Wintersteel'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Suriel may have sent [[Kiuran]] to remove [[Lindon]] from contention.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Suriel's visit was a significant event for [[Lindon]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c3">''Wintersteel'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Suriel once told [[Lindon]] that all [[Paths]] can be reduced to one: improve yourself.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17">''Wintersteel'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Suriel gave [[Lindon]] a second chance.<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c18">''Wintersteel'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|Suriel had a vision of the future showing something gigantic tramping [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c25">''Wintersteel'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|[[Judge Suriel]] is a favorite of someone mentioned by the [[Abidan]].<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28">''Wintersteel'', chapter 28</ref>}}

== Bloodline ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Possessing the [[Mantle of Suriel]], this entity can travel through the [[Way]] to enter an [[Iteration]] and use her presence to command the [[Abidan]]. During her interactions with [[Lindon]], she showed him an ending and experienced a vision of a future attack scheduled to occur thirty years later. After descending from heaven to rescue [[Lindon]] from death, she banished [[Li Markuth]] and demonstrated the ability to rewrite truth or turn back time. Within a space projected from her collected treasures, she appears with scripted eyes, armored fingertips, and vivid emerald hair. Although she is restricted by rules regarding her level of interference, she spends power to maintain the connection between the [[Way]] and the [[Iteration]] while using her mantle to revert worlds toward order. [[Lindon]] retains memories of her and carries a marble that burns with a blue flame, which she once commanded to begin a transmission.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel can tap into her mantle to revert a world towards a state of order.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c1">''Bloodline'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel can pass through the [[Way]] to enter an [[Iteration]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Suriel's Presence]] can issue commands to the [[Abidan]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel showed [[Lindon]] an ending.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c2">''Bloodline'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel had a vision of the future.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c5">''Bloodline'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel descended from heaven to bring [[Lindon]] back from death.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8">''Bloodline'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel saw an attack that was supposed to happen in thirty years.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Lindon]] has memories of Suriel.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11">''Bloodline'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel is someone who has died.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c11" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel banished [[Li Markuth]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c12">''Bloodline'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel has the power to turn back time or rewrite truth.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c16">''Bloodline'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel possesses a marble that [[Lindon]] rolls between his fingers.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18">''Bloodline'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel commanded a transmission to begin.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|[[Suriel's Presence]] spoke to her.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel's hair is bright and vivid emerald.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel's space is a projection of treasures she has collected.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel is restricted by rules regarding the degree of interference she can provide.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel's marble burns with a blue flame.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel has scripted eyes.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel has armored fingertips.<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel possesses the [[Mantle of Suriel]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19">''Bloodline'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|Suriel spends power to keep the world intact and maintain the connection between the [[Iteration]] and the [[Way]].<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c19" />}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|A young woman known to Ozriel for centuries, Suriel possesses bright green hair and eyes composed of spinning purple rings of script. She is marked by ghostly gray correlation lines extending from her knuckles to the back of her skull and maintains a connection to the [[Abidan network]] across existence. Clad in flowing armor, she utilizes her [[Razor]] to purge parasites and toxic energy while using her ability to look into [[Fate]] to glimpse the trade required for [[Makiel]]'s actions. After intervening to stabilize [[Iterations]] destabilized by the conflict between Ozriel and [[Makiel]], she fulfilled her promises to [[Lindon]] before vanishing within a rising tide of blue light.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel can connect to the network of [[Abidan]] all over existence.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3">''Reaper'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel has ghostly gray correlation lines running from the back of her skull to her knuckles.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Ozmanthus Arelius]] had no compatibility with the [[Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10">''Reaper'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel's marble contains light.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c10" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel is a new, young woman Ozriel had known for centuries.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c13">''Reaper'', chapter 13</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel has a [[Presence]] that lives inside her mind.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15">''Reaper'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel possesses armor that flows around her and runes that spin in her eyes.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel can look into [[Fate]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c15" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel uses her [[Razor]] to remove toxic energy and parasites.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22">''Reaper'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel glimpsed the trade that would be made for [[Makiel]]'s action.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel intervened when the conflict between Ozriel and [[Makiel]] destabilized surrounding [[Iterations]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c25">''Reaper'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|[[Lindon]] fulfilled everything Suriel promised him.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c26">''Reaper'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel has bright green hair and eyes that are bright purple rings of script.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c27">''Reaper'', chapter 27</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|Suriel vanished in a rising tide of blue light.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c27" />}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|A woman with bright green hair and a shining, multi-branched [[sword]], Suriel is a [[Judge]] tasked with the duty of repairing worlds. During her arrival at the [[Seat of the Accused]], she displayed a powerful [[Presence]] and noted that new methods had been developed to detect [[Judges]]. Her immense capabilities allow her to tear open rifts into the [[Way]], reconstitute entire continents from nothing, and perform the work of a full squad of lesser Phoenixes. Additionally, she serves as a caretaker for Ozriel, where she is responsible for observing him and ensuring he does not use his powers without permission.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel possesses [[Presence]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c1">''Dreadgod'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel transported herself to the [[Seat of the Accused]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel stated that something was made to detect [[Judges]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6">''Dreadgod'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel is a [[Judge]] who has the job of repairing worlds.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c10">''Dreadgod'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel is a woman with bright green hair and a shining [[sword]] split into many branches.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c20">''Dreadgod'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel is a caretaker for Ozriel.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24">''Dreadgod'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel is tasked with keeping Ozriel under observation and preventing him from using powers without permission.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel has the ability to reconstitute entire continents from nothing.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel can tear open a rift into the [[Way]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Suriel is capable of performing the work of an entire squad of lesser Phoenixes.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c24" />}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Floating in the emptiness above [[Vesper]], the Sixth [[Judge]] and [[Hound]] known as Suriel uses her [[Presence]] to signal obstacles within the [[Way]]. This Mantle-wearing healer possesses runic eyes capable of scanning the future, including a vision of the [[Wandering Titan]] wading through mountains. Her power manifests as blue, fiery wings and healing flames, and she carries a glass marble containing a single blue candle-flame that possesses a restorative [[aura]] and the ability to revive the dead. Though she rarely sits in judgment of mortals or ascended ones, Suriel is a figure capable of miracles who can locate anything in any world. During her travels, she wears a white uniform and eventually provides her marble to [[Lindon]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is a character who floats in the emptiness over [[Vesper]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c1">''Waybound'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is a character who uses her [[Presence]] to warn of barriers and obstacles in the [[Way]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c1" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel's marble has a restorative [[aura]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c2">''Waybound'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is the Sixth [[Judge]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel's power manifests as blue, fiery wings.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel has runic eyes that can scan the future.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is a [[Hound]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel has the ability to revive the dead.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c21">''Waybound'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is someone who might bring a miracle.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22">''Waybound'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is a character Ozriel mentions to [[Eithan]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is a healer who can fight in the Void and possesses a Mantle.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29">''Waybound'', chapter 29</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel has blue, healing fire.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel had a vision of the [[Wandering Titan]] wading through mountains.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c29" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel is a [[Judge]] who rarely sits in judgment of mortals or ascended ones.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32">''Waybound'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel might come back for [[Li Markuth]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c35">''Waybound'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel gave [[Lindon]] Suriel's marble.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c36">''Waybound'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel can find things anywhere in any world.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c36" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel wears a white uniform.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c37">''Waybound'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Suriel possesses a glass marble containing a single blue candle-flame.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c37" />}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|As a [[Judge]] and a leader of the [[Abidan]], Suriel is known as the [[Phoenix]]. She possesses the unique ability to restore lost eyes and currently holds an opening within the [[Sixth Division]]. Though she is considered too compassionate for her own good, she remains a vital ally to [[Lindon]] and a figure who may keep the [[Reaper]] alive. During her interactions, she appeared exasperated, even as [[Gadrael]] threatened to hand [[Cirian]] over to her.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel has an opening in the [[Sixth Division]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 7</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is considered too compassionate for her own good.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is an entity that can restore lost eyes.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Lindon]] has Suriel on his side.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is a figure who may keep the [[Reaper]] alive.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|[[Gadrael]] threatened to hand [[Cirian]] over to Suriel.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c7" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is the [[Phoenix]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c10">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is a [[Judge]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c12">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 12</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is a [[Judge]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c12" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is a leader of the [[Abidan]].<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 14</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|Suriel is one of the [[Judges]] and looked exasperated.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c14" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|As Suriel had once told him, “There are a million Paths in this world, Lindon, but any sage will tell you they can all be reduced to one. Improve yourself.”<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c17" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-08-wintersteel|"And tell Judge Suriel’s favorite I hope to see him too."<ref name="cradle-08-wintersteel-c28" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-09-bloodline|"Suriel descending from heaven to bring him back from death would have been the only miracle he ever received."<ref name="cradle-09-bloodline-c8" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|“That was made to detect Judges,” Suriel said.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c6" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|“Tell Suriel I’m counting on her,” Ozriel sent.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c22" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Unsouled]]
[[Category:Soulsmith]]
[[Category:Blackflame]]
[[Category:Skysworn]]
[[Category:Underlord]]
[[Category:Uncrowned]]
[[Category:Wintersteel]]
[[Category:Bloodline]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle characters]]
[[Category:Viewpoint characters]]
